Job Description: To advance the goals of DRC’s protection response program, the role will involve executing targeted protection activities designed to safeguard internally displaced persons (IDPs) and other conflict-affected communities, with a focus on robust case management, Cash for Protection (C4P+), psychosocial support, and crisis counseling, all in strict adherence to DRC-established guidelines and procedures. Additionally, the incumbent will assess and monitor case deadlines to ensure timely resolution and accountability.

Job Title: Training Coordinator for Resilience Building Initiatives
Job Description: The role involves maintaining comprehensive records of all training initiatives, assisting partners in producing high-quality training reports, and overseeing activity monitoring alongside supporting data collection efforts. The program is executing a strategic response that encompasses empowering local partners and their staff to adopt sustainable methodologies for delivering humanitarian aid. This includes investing in skill-building initiatives to enhance the capacity of Myanmar’s civil society in adopting ‘person-centered’ and ‘trauma-informed’ approaches, aimed at addressing staff stress and trauma, as well as community-based resilience training. Strengthening this understanding will help mitigate risks of harm or re-traumatization among individuals and communities. To achieve this, Christian Aid seeks to appoint a coordinator who will collaborate closely with partners and staff to uphold the integrity of the project during its six-month duration (July 1, 2026 – December 31, 2026). Under the guidance of the Program Manager (Christian Aid) and the Project Focal Point in Christian Aid Myanmar, the Training Coordinator will work alongside the broader CA Myanmar team, the Tear fund Myanmar team, CRM trainers, and partner organizations to coordinate, facilitate, and ensure the excellence and documentation of CRM training implementation and learning outcomes to Stage 3 of the initiative. Christian Aid operates as a prominent member of the ACT Alliance, holding charity registrations in England and Wales (No. 1105851), Scotland (No. SC039150), and Ireland (No. NIC101631), as well as company registrations in the UK and ROI (No. 5171525 and NI059154, respectively).
Company Details:
Myanmar is currently grappling with a multifaceted humanitarian emergency fueled by ongoing conflict, mass displacement, and recent natural disasters. This crisis has led to widespread psychological strain and restricted access to mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), particularly in rural and conflict-affected regions. Frontline responders and local humanitarian workers are disproportionately impacted, facing elevated levels of stress, burnout, and trauma. Although awareness of these challenges has grown, there is still a notable absence of standardized, scalable mechanisms to address staff wellbeing and duty of care within organizations.

In response, Christian Aid and Tear fund are spearheading a Collective Initiative to scale the Community Resilience Model (CRM), a peer-led, evidence-based framework designed to enhance resilience among both staff and communities. As the initiative expands to encompass community-level implementation, a Training Coordinator is needed to facilitate the seamless coordination, execution, and documentation of CRM trainings across participating organizations.

Job Description: The Individual Consultant will report to the Regional Director of the UNESCO Regional Office in Bangkok and the Office for UN Coordination in Asia and the Pacific, with direct oversight from the Head of the UNESCO Project Antenna Office in Yangon. Collaboration with the UNESCO Education Team will be essential to fulfill the following responsibilities: supporting the analysis of household survey datasets by organizing, analyzing, and presenting statistical data through tables and visualizations; conducting desk reviews and background research on Myanmar’s education sector, covering access, participation, continuity, equity, vulnerability, service delivery, and crisis-related learning barriers, including the identification, synthesis, and summarization of relevant literature, policy documents, and research findings; aiding in the design and development of analytical frameworks for UNESCO’s education assessments, such as indicator and data source mapping, methodological documentation, and the preparation of technical background materials; contributing to the creation of research reports, presentations, briefing notes, and other knowledge products derived from analysis and survey activities; and assisting with data documentation, quality assurance, and coordination with team members and partners as needed.
Company Details: UNESCO Myanmar’s Project Antenna Office is executing a multi-year initiative under the Global Partnership for Education (GPE) System Capacity Grant (SCG) for Myanmar. The program aims to enhance the resilience and efficiency of the country’s education sector by strengthening planning, coordination, financing, monitoring, and evidence-based policymaking. The SCG plays a key role in improving sector coordination, strategic planning, and data-driven decision-making across Myanmar’s education landscape. Given the dynamic and complex operating environment, high-quality, timely data analysis is critical to guide education programming, facilitate policy dialogue, mobilize resources, and track sector performance. To address these needs, UNESCO seeks specialized technical assistance in processing, analyzing, and presenting quantitative and secondary data to inform education sector planning, coordination, monitoring, and evidence generation—including reviewing and systematizing large datasets.

Job Description: Under the Project Manager’s supervision and in close collaboration with Project Officers, the Project Coordinator will be responsible for strengthening the referral mechanism by conducting regular supervision and monitoring visits to Community-Based TB Care (CBTBC) volunteers and Field Agents across designated townships. They will facilitate seamless coordination between TB volunteers, the National TB Program (NTP), local health departments, general practitioners, and pharmacies to ensure the timely referral of presumptive TB cases for diagnosis and treatment. Additionally, the Project Coordinator will lead and coordinate teleconsultation services in partnership with private radiology centers and project medical staff to deliver TB services directly from the office (PSA). They will also oversee the adherence to TB infection control protocols at all teleconsultation sites (PSA) and arrange for laboratory testing of sputum samples collected and referred from designated collection centers. The role involves managing the disbursement of performance-based incentives for TB volunteers and enhancing collaboration between TB volunteers and Basic Health Staff (BHS) at the township level. In terms of technical oversight and capacity building, the Project Coordinator will supervise and provide hands-on technical guidance to TB volunteers in implementing CBTBC activities, support their efforts in advocacy and health education sessions at the community level, and deliver on-the-job training on the referral of presumptive TB cases and follow-up procedures. They will also assist volunteers in maintaining accurate recording and reporting of CBTBC activities to the respective township health departments. Finally, the Project Coordinator will foster cooperation and coordination among all stakeholders involved.

Sun Community Health is recruiting a Data Assistant to support the TB team by fulfilling the following duties: managing the preparation and documentation of reports for team initiatives; collaborating with the Medical Doctor (Team Leader) and Medical Assistant to plan and execute activities in alignment with the established work plan and targeted project outcomes; supporting the Medical Doctor and Medical Assistant in delivering TB services in accordance with established guidelines and project objectives; ensuring sufficient stock of essential supplies is maintained as outlined in the team schedule; assisting with reporting and record-keeping for the Medical Doctor and Medical Assistant; conducting health education sessions and providing counseling services for TB and related conditions; and performing any other tasks as assigned.

Applicants must hold a graduate degree and possess a certified nurse aide qualification. Prior to work experience in a relevant field is strongly preferred.

The role involves assisting in the delivery of TB services according to approved guidelines and project objectives, ensuring proper consumption and tracking of necessary supplies on a monthly basis. Job Description:
Sun Community Health is searching for a Team Leader to oversee tuberculosis (TB) control initiatives by fostering collaboration with Sun Providers, health authorities, local administrative bodies, and the SCH implementation team. This role involves planning, executing, training, managing, and supervising TB control programs in alignment with organizational objectives. The position requires ensuring TB team operations adhere to established work plans while meeting the predetermined targets for assigned projects. Additionally, the Team Leader must deliver TB services in strict accordance with approved guidelines and project goals.

Responsibilities include supervising team members during field operations, maintaining financial accountability for the team’s activities in compliance with organizational policies, and managing project supplies by submitting stock consumption reports monthly to the relevant supervisor. The role also entails conducting regular data analysis to implement timely corrective measures, reviewing and verifying data, and submitting standardized reports promptly to designated units and supervisors.

Furthermore, the Team Leader will organize quarterly review meetings in project townships and participate in necessary project assessments. They will conduct regular monitoring and supportive visits to all designated Sun clinics within their assigned area to distribute social franchising products, distribute updated forms, collect Monthly Clinic Reports (MCRs), process financial transactions for provider incentive payments, clinic assistance fees, and client incentives. The position also involves supporting Sun providers in meeting social franchising and clinical practice standards, as well as conducting advocacy efforts and area mapping.

Job Description: As the primary liaison in the field, you will serve as the key point of contact between private clinics, township and district health departments, implementing partners, and the central office. You will oversee the day-to-day execution of project activities across clinics, community sites, and township health departments. This includes disseminating schedules, activity plans, and meeting updates to field teams and partners. Additionally, you will assist in organizing trainings, review meetings, and community outreach initiatives. Building and maintaining strong working relationships with local health authorities, community organizations, and private providers will be essential. You will support community-level activities as needed, ensuring alignment with township health priorities and project objectives. When required, you will assist in supervising community initiatives such as awareness sessions, mobilization efforts, and referral processes. Providing basic technical and operational support will be part of your role to facilitate smooth field implementation. You will collect service delivery data, activity reports, and monitoring forms from clinics, communities, and township teams, verifying their completeness and accuracy before forwarding them to the central office. Data and activity reports will be compiled into standardized templates and submitted punctually. At the township level, you will maintain simple filing systems for reference and audits. You will gather and review drug and supply usage reports from clinics, assist in preparing and submitting drug indent requests in the event of low stock, and monitor facility stock balances to identify and report risks of shortages or overstock. Furthermore, you will help maintain basic inventory registers and ensure compliance with stock management protocols.

Job Description: The Community Mobilizer is responsible for conducting monitoring visits to community volunteers, auxiliary midwives (Amos), and both formal pharmacy channels and informal ‘drug gist’ channels within their assigned area to distribute health-related products, distribute updated forms, IEC materials, and promotional materials, collect MCRs, and disburse provider incentive payments. They ensure that all SCH distributors of health-related products maintain adequate stock levels and adhere to acceptable storage conditions, including proper expiration dates, while monitoring sales and consultation volumes. Additionally, the role involves advocating with Sun doctors, public/private laboratories, and affiliated X-ray centers for case referrals. The Community Mobilizer also participates in awareness-raising activities for assigned projects, such as MNCH, SRHR, Malaria, and TB, and supports health education sessions on family care practices, including MNCH, immunization, infant/young child feeding, sanitation, hygiene, and COVID-19 (where applicable to MNCH/SRHR projects). They distribute clean delivery kits and family newborn kits to pregnant women and first aid kits to the community as needed (for MNCH/SRHR projects specifically). The position is responsible for disseminating technical updates to community volunteers, Amos, and both formal and informal health product distributors in their assigned areas and addressing any issues or challenges that arise with these stakeholders.

Job Title: Proposal for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises
Job Description:
A Statement of Work will outline specific deliverables, milestones, and a mutually agreed-upon budget, detailing the obligations of both parties involved. The support will be formalized through a Cost Reimbursable Subaward, where the subrecipient is reimbursed for allowable, incurred expenses necessary to execute the subaward activities, not exceeding the allocated funds. Key conditions include a maximum award duration of 12 months, a mandatory Pre-Award Assessment, and the requirement for financial reporting. The agreement will incorporate the transfer of relevant donor policies, compliance with Mercy Corps’ Code of Conduct, and several due diligence measures to confirm the subrecipient’s capacity to meet the subaward terms, absence of conflicts of interest, and eligibility. Additional requirements specify that no profit may be included in the subaward budget, donor-restricted goods may not be procured under the agreement, applicable tax obligations will be evaluated and determined, and a draft agreement is provided for review.

Eligible applicants for the Request for Applications (RFA) include legally registered private sector entities, particularly arises. Applicants must meet the following criteria: legal registration, current operations in Boggle/Laputan or a concrete plan to expand services into the region, demonstrated financial and operational capability, a commitment to gender equality and inclusion, and adherence to environmental sustainability standards.
Company Details: Mercy Corps Myanmar, via the Climate Resilience of Mangrove-Dependent Agri-food Systems through Market, Household and Community Transformations (CRAFT) initiative, invites private sector agricultural enterprises to submit Requests for Applications (RFA's) for participation in a dedicated Partnership Facility. This facility is designed to broaden access to climate-resilient, inclusive, and sustainable agricultural services in the Ayeyarwady Delta. The RFA serves as the initial phase in selecting private sector partners who are dedicated to introducing or scaling innovative, climate-smart, and gender-responsive market solutions tailored to benefit smallholder farmers and mangrove-dependent households in Boggle and Laputan Townships. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to engage in a collaborative co-design process with Mercy Corps to refine a detailed intervention model and formalize a partnership agreement. Program Overview: Through this initiative, the CRAFT program seeks to empower target households and communities in Boggle and Laputan townships to enhance their climate resilience.

Job Description:
The Procurement Associate will execute logistics and procurement activities under MRCS programs in compliance with MRCS and donor procurement guidelines. They will maintain an up-to-date database of local suppliers for high-demand items, track market trends, and verify that all procured goods meet specified quality standards and, when applicable, possess valid certifications. In collaboration with project and technical teams, the Associate will develop and regularly update the procurement plan. They will solicit quotations, conduct comparative bid analyses, and present purchasing recommendations for approval before procuring goods and services through standardized procedures. Additionally, the Associate will engage with suppliers and service providers to ensure punctual and high-quality delivery of goods and services, working closely with the MRCS procurement and logistics teams.

To facilitate seamless operations, they will coordinate with internal stakeholders to guarantee timely supply delivery to distribution sites and promptly report any delays or changes. In logistics, the Associate will compile and distribute weekly stock position and transportation reports, including logistics movement updates, to relevant headquarters departments. They will arrange for the secure and efficient transport of procured items to beneficiaries and disaster-affected regions, ensuring adherence to MRCS warehousing protocols prior to final distribution. The Associate will also plan and coordinate transportation needs, including MRCS fleet vehicles and third-party logistics providers, while supporting the Senior Logistics Officer in these efforts.
Company Details: The Logistics and Supply Chain Management Department plays a key role in facilitating relief distribution, managing transport operations, procuring relief items locally, supporting healthcare initiatives, and strengthening disaster management programs—including the acquisition of preparedness supplies. The department also oversees customs clearance for imported relief goods, disseminates operational procedures and guidelines, and provides training to staff and volunteers. Since early 2013, the team has been assisting the Myanmar Red Cross Society (MRCS) in developing a unified community-based approach to enhance local resilience. Additionally, it provides technical assistance to improve disaster preparedness and response systems as outlined in the operational plan. The Logistics and Supply Chain Management Team supports these efforts, as well as other MRCS programs implemented nationwide with bilateral support from Partner National Societies (PNSs) in Myanmar. The Logistics Officer will be responsible for initiating and executing logistics activities.

Job Description:
The incumbent will ensure the overall quality of project deliverables while fostering capacity development among staff, RCVs, and local communities in program execution and implementation. Additionally, they will collaborate with relevant stakeholders to establish and support a village-level water repair and maintenance system. Acting as a vital bridge between the project and affected communities at the grassroots level, the Field Officer will oversee the implementation of project activities in adherence to standardized methodologies, employing participatory approaches to enhance inclusivity, consultation, coordination, and information-sharing, thereby maximizing stakeholder engagement. The role involves providing hands-on training to volunteers and organizing regular meetings with volunteers and community committees. Persons with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply, as equal opportunity will be provided. Our recruitment and selection processes are designed with a commitment to safeguarding children and preventing violence among community members within our programs.

Guided by the principles of Humanity, Impartiality, Neutrality, Independence, Voluntary Service, Unity, and Universality, the Field Officer will work closely with the MRCS Field Manager, Field Officers, field teams, volunteers, and local authorities to establish, equip, train, and support communities in target areas. The incumbent may also be tasked with additional responsibilities as assigned.
Company Details:
Society (MRCS) is a specialized emergency response initiative established to meet critical humanitarian needs arising from the recent earthquake affecting multiple regions across Myanmar. In coordination with domestic and international partners, the operation is focused on delivering prompt and efficient assistance across vital sectors, including shelter, health, water and sanitation, livelihood recovery, and protection services. Managed under the Mandalay Earthquake Relief and Recovery Operation (ERROR) Team, the initiative collaborates closely with MRCS Internal Departments, Branches, Volunteers, and Communities to ensure a cohesive, community-centered response in alignment with MRCS’s strategic priorities and operational guidelines.

Position Purpose: The Field Officer will ensure all activities are executed in accordance with the project’s goals, objectives, and output indicators. The Field Officer will be based in Sagging and will report directly to the [specified supervisor, if applicable].
